The floodwaters rush through my chest without slowing or feeling the cold.
I watch the living scream as the earth splits, unable to pull them from the rubble.
My home was swept away by the wind, yet I still drift where the walls once stood.
I try to warn the fleeing crowd, but my voice is only a shiver in the air.
The fire consumes the timber and bone, but it cannot burn what is already gone.
The shimmering light of that dying star ripples right through my translucent chest.
I feel a strange vibration in my essence that echoes the pulse of the void.
Even without breath, I find myself staring in awe at the fracturing of the heavens.
My tether to this plane feels fragile as the celestial alignment tears the sky open.
I drift through the viewing platform, unseen by the living who tremble at the cosmic fire.
My spectral form drifts through the new fence as if the cedar planks were merely thin mist.
This living man claims the orchard, yet I remember planting these seeds centuries before his bloodline existed.
I shall howl through the floorboards of the survey office until they restore the original line of my estate.
It matters little who holds the deed when I can slide through the locked gates of the manor.
The boundary markers have shifted in the soil, but the chill of my presence remains fixed upon the east ridge.
I drift through the shimmering edge of the rift, feeling my essence stretch across dimensions.
The pull of the portal is stronger than the lingering ties that bind me here.
My translucent form flickers as the swirling energy of the gateway threatens to dissolve my spirit.
I wonder if the world beyond this threshold holds a way to regain my flesh.
The cold wind of the void whispers secrets that only the dead can truly comprehend.
These thick brambles do not hinder my stride, for I slip through the thorns without feeling their sting.
I drift above the damp loam, watching the fog coil around the ancient trunks like a heavy shroud.
The living travelers stumble through the underbrush, unaware that I am gliding silently beside them in the gloom.
I reach out to touch a towering fern, but my fingers pass through the green fronds like wind.
There is no path for one who does not touch the earth, yet I know every hidden hollow here.
I drift through these hollow corridors, listening for a vibration that never comes.
The dust settles upon my translucent skin while the air remains perfectly still.
I have forgotten the sound of my own voice in this oppressive quiet.
Centuries have dissolved into a single breath of silence that will not break.
I reach for a memory of noise, but the void swallows every gesture.
Your blade passes through my chest like wind through a willow tree.
You cannot banish what has already crossed the threshold of the grave.
I will haunt your every waking hour until your spirit joins mine.
Your threats are meaningless to one who no longer fears the dying.
I shall drift through these walls and watch you tremble in darkness.
My fingers pass right through the gold watch I spent forty years winding.
I try to clutch this velvet jewelry box, but it slips through my essence.
The heavy silver trophy remains frozen in time while I drift through its metal.
It kills me that I can see my favorite book but cannot turn a page.
I hover over this porcelain doll, longing to feel the weight of it once more.
My spectral energy is fading and I must decide which rooms to haunt.
I can only manifest for a few minutes before my essence thins too much.
This ancient anchor holds a finite amount of tether to the mortal plane.
I must ration my screams if I wish to terrify the new tenants.
There is barely enough ectoplasm left to rattle these chains for one more night.
